mnemonics.title: CNN Pipeline Order
mnemonics.content: Remember: C-P-C-P-F - Convolution, Pooling, Convolution, Pooling, Fully Connected. Classic CNN architecture flows through these layers repeatedly before classification.

mnemonics.title: Padding Formula
mnemonics.content: Same Padding: P = (K-1)/2 where K is kernel size. For 3x3 kernel: P=1; For 5x5 kernel: P=2; For 7x7 kernel: P=3. Keep P=0 for Valid padding.

mnemonics.title: Output Size Calculation
mnemonics.content: O = floor((I - K + 2P)/S) + 1. Remember: Input minus Kernel, add 2×Padding, divide by Stride, then add 1.

acronyms.usage: Deep learning architecture for image processing using convolution operations
Layer connecting all neurons to every neuron in the previous layer for classification
Activation function commonly used in CNN: f(x) = max(0, x)

acronyms.expansion: kernel slides this many pixels at a time
acronyms.usage: Determines how much the filter moves each step; higher stride = smaller output
Preserves spatial dimensions (same) or allows edge feature extraction (valid)

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a flashlight (kernel) sliding across a dark image. At each position, it multiplies the light with underlying pixels and sums them up - creating a new brightness map (feature map).
Think of pooling as zoom-out: Max pooling keeps the brightest/highest activated pixels (like highlighters), while average pooling blurs to show general intensity.
3x3 filters can detect: horizontal edges, vertical edges, diagonal edges, textures - each filter creates its own activation map highlighting specific patterns.
Stack multiple 2D feature maps vertically to form a 3D volume - depth equals number of filters, spatial dimensions shrink with pooling.

analogies.analogy: Like a rubber stamp moving across paper - at each position, the stamp's pattern (kernel) combines with the paper (input) to create an impression (feature map).
Like creating a thumbnail from a high-res photo - you lose detail but reduce size and become more resilient to small changes (noise).
Think of filters as different experts: one sees only edges, one sees textures, one sees colors - each specializes in detecting specific patterns.

analogies.anal analogy: Like standing at different distances from a painting: close up you see brushstrokes (fine details), far away you see the overall scene (abstract features).
